( Arith .) The square of a number. barg-karman , S. ( Math .) An operation relating to square numbers. barg-mūl , S. ( Math .) Square root.

more: s. Name of a small round fruit.

def: n. m. A sweet and acid fruit of yellowish red and nearly round. Artocarpus lakoocha . Wat.
v. a. To bring forward, to lead on (an army).

more: God, the cause and essence of the world from which all things are supposed to proceed and to which they return. برهم استر brahm-astra, s. m. A fabled weapon, which, consecrated by a formula addressed to Brahmā, deals infallible destruction to those against whom it is discharged. برهمبهوج brahm-bhoj, Feeding of Brāhmaṇs. برهم ديت brahma-daitya, An apparition, Satan, ghost. برهمراتر ब्रह्मरात्रि brahma-rātri, s. f. A night of Brahmā, comprising a thousand ages of the gods, or 216,000,000 of those of mortals. See کلپ kalpa, sign. 2. برهم راکهس brahma-rākhas, A kind of demon. برهم سيش brahm-sesh̤, The leavings of Brāhmaṇs. برهم هتيا brahma-hatyā, The murder of a Brāhmaṇ; sacrilege. برهمهتيارا brahma-hatyā-rā, s. m. A murderer of a Brāhmaṇ. برهم هتياري brahma-hatyā-rī, s. f. A murderess of a Brāhmaṇ.

adj. 1. Confused, entangled, spoiled. 2. Angry, vexed, sullen. برهم درهم bar-ham darham, adj. Entangled, confused, topsyturvy. برهم زدن barham-zadan, s. m. Confusing, mixing together, striking together. برهم زده barbam-zada, Confused, convulsed, turned topsyturvy. برهم زدن barham-zan, An exciter of quarrels, an embroiler. برهم زني barham-zan-ī, s. f. 1. Embroiling, confusing. 2. Interference, prevention, putting a stop to.

s.f. Confusing; embroiling; interference, prevention, putting a stop to
v.t. To throw into confusion or disorder; to embroil, complicate; to mar, ruin, destroy.

def: adj. 1. Entangled; jumbled; confused. 2. Wrath; irate; angry; enraged. darham-barham; H. tittar-bittar , adv. Topsy turvy.
more: n. m. 1. The Supreme Being; the all-pervading; the self-existent; the absolute; the divine cause and essence of the world, from which all things are supposed to proceed and to which they return. 2. Spirit; soul. 3. The sacred text; Scripture (the Veds). 4. A Brāhman.

def: s.f. Confusion, convulsion, trouble, anarchy; vexation, displeasure, anger, wrath.
more: s. f. Confusion, convulsion, trouble, anarchy.

ety: baṛhā˚ = baṛhāw˚ = Prk. वड्ढावे(इ) or वड्ढाव(इ) = S. वर्ध + आपय(ति), rt. वृध् with caus. aug. आपि
def: v.t. To increase, enlarge, extend, lengthen, produce, prolong, stretch, draw out; to advance, promote, raise, exalt, aggrandize; to amplify, magnify, exaggerate; to move forward, move on, carry forward, move away, remove (as a table-cloth), close (a shop, &c.), stop (a child's milk, e.g. dūdh baṛhānā, 'to wean'); to postpone, delay; to extinguish (a lamp)
more: (trans. of بڙهنا) v. a. 1. To increase, augment, enlarge, to lengthen, to make advance, to forward, to exalt, elevate, to raise, to promote, aggrandize, rear up, extend, stretch out, amplify, dilate. 2. To extinguish. 3. To shut up (a shop). 4. To remove the tablecloth.
subs: v.t. To bring forward, lead on (an army, &c.).

ety: baṛh˚ = Prk. वड्ढ(इ) = S. वर्द्ध(त), rt. वृध्
def: v.n. To increase, enlarge, be enhanced, be extended, be produced, be prolonged; to extend, lengthen; to grow, rise; to swell (as a stream); to be raised, be elevated, be exalted, be promoted; to prosper, flourish, thrive, become rich or powerful, acquire strength or dignity; to gain, acquire (as profit); to go on, proceed, advance, make head; to get beyond, surpass, exceed (with abl.); to be set or put aside; to be cut off, be stopped (as a supply, &c.; e.g. dūdh baṛh-jānā, 'to be weaned'), to be removed or taken away (as a table-cloth); to be closed (as a shop); to be extinguished (as a lamp); to be lowered, to fall (as a rate or price)
more: v. n. To increase, enlarge, go on, proceed, advance, exaggerate, grow, rise, swell, to be promoted, to amount, lengthen.
subs: v.n. Intens. of, and = baṛhnā, q.v.
baṛh-ke ćalnā, v.n. To be arrogant, proud, &c. = itrānā, q.v.
ex: for one's hand to be stretched out
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀟𑁆𑀠𑀤𑀺 (vaḍḍhadi), from Sanskrit वर्धते (vardhate, “to grow, increase”). Cognate to Punjabi ਵੱਧਣਾ (vaddhaṇā) / وَدھݨا (vadhṇā), Marathi वाढणे (vāḍhṇe), Assamese বঢ়া (borha).

def: v. n. S. वर्ध्दन Pr. vaḍḍhaī , from वृध् to increase. 1. To increase (in dimensions); rise; grow. 2. To stretch; enlarge; widen; expand. 3. To grow long; lengthen; to be produced, prolonged. 4. To swell; overflow. Daryā baṛhā huā hai . 5. To be propagated, multiplied, extended. 6. To be elevated or raised high (a kite). 7. ( Mus .) Crescendo; forte. 8. To remain over and above. 9. To go forward; advance; proceed; gain ground; go or move on. Āge baṛho . 10. To rise (in price). Āj kal bhāo baṛhā huā hai . 11. To gain; acquire (as profit). Kaho, kyā baṛhā? 12. To outstep; go ahead; go beyond; outstrip; exceed; surpass. 13. To be promoted, exalted. Darjah baṛhnā . 14. To prosper; thrive; flourish. Rām baṛhāë sab baṛhe, bal-kar baṛhā na koë, Bal kar-ke Rāvan baṛhā, chhin meṅ ḍālā khoë! Dohā. From Rām alone can increase come, all human strength is vain, How in a moment Rāvans's pride all in the dust was lain! 15. To become rich, or powerful; acquire strength or dignity. 16. To become cheap; fall in price. Fasal se pān ser gehūṅ baṛh gaë . 16. To be put aside. Pujāpā baṛhnā . 17. To be shut up (a shop). Dūkān baṛhnā . 18. To be extinguished or put out (a lamp). Chirāg baṛh gayā . khānah-i-jamā se baṛhnā , v. n. To exceed the amount at one's credit; to overdraw. baṛh-ke chal'nā, yā baṛh-chalnā , v. n. To be proud. See اِترانا kamtī baṛhtī . A little more or less; about.
v. n. See بڙهنا baṛhnā.
s.f. Increase, growth, prosperity, advancement, rise; advance (paid for manufacture, cultivation, or contract); — broom, brush.
more: s. f. 1. A broom. 2. Advance made for cultivation or manufacture or a contract.

n. An epithet of the Deity; the Creator. bārī-tā, n. m. The Most High God.
W; n. f. 1. A caste of men who sew together broad leaves which do duty for plates ( pattal ). 2. (H. bālnā to light) A torch bearer by profession (Tirhut).

n. f. S. वाटी 1. Homestead; homestall; home; hearth. 2. A plantation; field; orchard; a kitchen garden. 3. A cotton field. W. bāṛī chugnā,—bīnnā , v. a. To pluck or gather the fruit or vegetables in a field; gather the cotton crop. (W.). Terā jījā ghar naīṅ, sāthan, Bāṛī chugan na jāūṅgī . W. Rus. Wom. Song. Your brother-in-law, my dear, is not at home, To pick the cotton field, I cannot come.
(from برأ Heb. Created) part. act. (used substantively) The Deity, the Creator. باري تعالي barī-taʼāﻌlạ̄, The most high God.

v. n. H. baurānā to be distracted. 1. To murmur; mutter (chiefly in sleep or delirium). 1. Ūṅṭ baṛṛātā hī ladtā hai . Prov. Just as to load the camel you begin, His rattling water-works all groan within. (Said of those who grumble when they are required to do any work). 2. Yūṅ to kah saktā nahīṅ maiṅ hāl-i-dil us se, magar, Makr se so jāūṅ, aur jo dil meṅ hai baṛṛāūṅ meṅ . Mārūf. To her I durst not of my heart's griefs make lament, But feigning sleep I might in murmurs give them vent. 2. W. To starve; die of want; to be famine-stricken. Kāl ke māre parjā baṛṛā gaī .

def: v.t. To cause to rain; to shower down, pour down; to scatter, sprinkle; to winnow.
more: v. a. Caus. of برسنا q. v. 1. Mehr kare to meṅh barsāve . Prov. If God is gracious it will rain. 2. Pair gahā jo rākhe pās, bin barsāë na pāve rās . Agric. Prov. The trodden but unwinnowed grain With all its husk will still remain. 3. Jo tum haṅsne meṅ ho murshid, to maiṅ rone meṅ hūṅ kāmil, Tumheṅ bijlī girānā, aur mujhe barsānā ātā hai . Ashk. If you of laughter be a queen, in weeping I'm perfection, If yours the lightning flash, in me behold the rain's reflection.
(causal of برسنا q. v.) v. a. To cause to rain, to shower down.

def: s.f. Rainy season, the rains (the third season, of the six, from the fifteenth of Ásārh to the fifteenth of Bhādoṅ); rain (in general).
more: n. f. S. वर्षर्तु from वर्ष rain ऋतु season. The rainy season; the third season (of the six) from the 15th of Āsāṛh to 15th Bhādoṅ . 1. Inhīṅ āṅkhoṅ se barsāt ṭer karoge? Mazāq. Jest. Do you expect to go through the rainy season with those diminutive eyes? (Said to one whose eyes are very small or who has been once deceived by his eyes. 2. Barsāt meṅ kaṛhāī ghar ghar . Prov. All in the season when the rains come down, In every house the frypan fizzes on. 3. Barsāt kā jahān meṅ lashkar phisal paṛā, Bādal bhī har taraf se havā par phisal paṛā. Barsāt . Rains. Nazīr. The rains their armed hosts upon the earth have hurled, The clouds likewise the air hem in, around it whirled. 4. Barsāt bar sāt . Prov. Saying. The rains are best enjoyed with one's beloved.
s. f. The rainy season, the rains.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀲𑀸𑀭𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺 (vāsāratti), from Sanskrit वर्षारात्रि (varṣārātri, “the rainy season”). Cognate with Gujarati વરસાદ (varsād).

(The vendor's praise of the durability of his goods). 2. Joban jātā jāntī, bhar letī hūṅ jhāl, Thoṛā thoṛā barattī, nūn, tel kī ḍhāl . Wom. Dohā. So fleeting youthful charms! had I but only known! Within a basket all enclosed they had not flown, As sparingly as salt and oil they had been shown. 2. To spend; expend.

Rus. v. n. H. apras . To get leprosy. Dūr! utte ke, dūr ho! dūr! pare! kam-jāt! Terī deh kīṛe paṛo, baṛaṭ jāo sab gāt! Wom. Play. Away! begone! miles hence! away! avaunt, base born! May worms your body eat, a loathsome leper lorn!
